action.reasoning: Observation: The current best test balanced_accuracy is 0.8539 from iteration 16 (ensemble_fine.pt). Training additional ConvNeXt backbones with different seeds/losses has not improved the ensemble beyond this. Analysis: The best backbone is still the iteration 9 ConvNeXt-Tiny trained with natural sampling. Its classifier head is heavily biased toward head classes. Classifier retraining (cRT) on frozen features should remove this bias, but the previous cRT attempt (iter 10) was likely under-trained or unstable. By caching backbone features (~10k vectors) and training a new linear head for 100 epochs with balanced sampling in seconds, we can do cRT properly. We will then ensemble this cRT model with the original best_iter9.pt via TTA-aware logit-adjustment grid search. This is fast (<5 min) and could push the score above 0.8539. Decision: Write retrain_head.py to extract features from best_iter9.pt, retrain the head with balanced sampling, tune tau, save the model, then immediately run ensemble fine-tuning against best_iter9.pt, smoke-test, and submit eval.
action.command: python retrain_head.py --ckpt checkpoints/best_iter9.pt --output checkpoints/crt_v2.pt && python tune_ensemble_fine.py --ckpt1 checkpoints/best_iter9.pt --ckpt2 checkpoints/crt_v2.pt --output checkpoints/ensemble_crt.pt && mkdir -p fake_test && cp /data/val.pt fake_test/test.pt && python predict.py --data_path fake_test --checkpoint checkpoints/ensemble_crt.pt --output fake_out.json && python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('fake_out.json')); print('Smoke test OK, preds count:', len(d['predictions']))"
